Need help with a license or permit bond? Explore our License & Permit Bond Hub for state-specific requirements and application guides.

The Basics

What Is a Wisconsin Money Transmitter Bond?

A Wisconsin Money Transmitter Bond is a state-required surety bond that protects consumers from financial loss if a money transmitter fails to follow Wisconsin regulations. This bond applies to businesses that:

✓
Transmit money or monetary value across state lines
✓
Sell or issue payment instruments
✓
Operate digital wallets or stored-value platforms
✓
Provide app-based or online money transfer services

The bond ensures compliance with the Wisconsin Department of Financial Institutions.

Pricing

Wisconsin Money Transmitter Bond Cost

Wisconsin sets the bond amount based on transaction volume and business risk, typically ranging from $50,000 to $500,000.

50K-500K
Bond Amount
1%-5%
Annual Premium Rate
Wisconsin Department of Financial Institutions
Regulating Agency

Your premium depends on:

✓
Bond amount
✓
Credit score
✓
Financial strength
✓
Business experience

Wisconsin-ready issuance - fast approvals and digital delivery for immediate filing with the state.

Eligibility

Who Needs a Wisconsin Money Transmitter Bond?

You need this bond if your business:

✓
Sends or receives money on behalf of customers
✓
Issues payment instruments such as money orders
✓
Provides digital or electronic transfer services
✓
Handles stored-value or prepaid access products
✓
Operates as a licensed money services business

Any company applying for a Wisconsin money transmitter license must maintain an active bond throughout the licensing period.

Requirements

Wisconsin Money Transmitter Requirements

Wisconsin requires:

✓
NMLS application
✓
Audited financial statements
✓
Background and credit checks
✓
Net-worth documentation
✓
Surety bond filing
✓
Compliance with Wisconsin Department of Financial Institutions regulations
Licensing

Wisconsin Money Transmitter License

To obtain your license, you must:

✓
Apply through NMLS
✓
Secure the required surety bond
✓
Submit audited financials
✓
Provide business plans and compliance documents
✓
Pass background and credit checks
Simple Process

How the Money Transmitter Bond Process Works

1

Complete the Application

Submit the quick application. Fast Wisconsin approval - your bond is issued to meet all Wisconsin Department of Financial Institutions requirements.

2

Secure the Lowest Rate

We match you with the best rate available from A-rated sureties.

3

Receive Your Bond

Same-day issuance available for NMLS or state filing.

Questions

Top Money Transmitter Bond Questions Answered

Our most common questions answered efficiently.

How much does a money transmitter bond cost?

Most qualified applicants pay 1 to 5 percent of the bond amount annually, with pricing based on bond amount, credit score, financial strength, and business experience in Wisconsin.

How long does approval take?

Approvals are fast, with same-day issuance available for NMLS or Wisconsin state filing.

What does the bond guarantee?

The bond guarantees compliance with Wisconsin Department of Financial Institutions regulations and protects consumers from financial loss.

Can the bond be refunded?

Refund policies vary by surety. Contact us for specifics on your Wisconsin money transmitter bond.

Do I need a separate bond for each state?

Yes, money transmitter bonds are state-specific and each state has individual requirements.
